deep_dive.title: DREAM-S: Speculative Decoding with Searchable Drafting and Target-Aware Refinement for Multimodal Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding (SD) has proven to be an effective technique for accelerating autoregressive generation in large language models (LLMs) however, its application to vision-language models (VLMs) remains relatively unexplored. We propose~\textit{DREAM-S}, a novel SD framework designed specifically for fast and efficient decoding in VLMs. DREAM-S leverages a neural architecture search (NAS) framework with target-aware supernet training to automatically identify both the optimal interaction strategy between the draft and target models, and the most suitable draft model architecture for the underlying hardware implementation platform. DREAM-S additionally incorporates adaptive intermediate feature distillation, guided by attention entropy, to enable efficient draft training. Experiments on a range of well-established VLMs show that DREAM-S achieves up to a $3.85\times$ speedup compared to standard decoding approaches and significantly outperforms existing SD baselines. The code is publicly available at: https://github.com/SAI-Lab-NYU/DREAM-S .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: speculative decoding for multimodal generation including VLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 应用于视觉语言模型的投机解码
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 应用于视觉语言模型的投机解码
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 应用于视觉语言模型的投机解码
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DREAM-S is a speculative decoding framework tailored for VLMs, using neural architecture search to jointly optimize draft-target interaction and draft model architecture, along with adaptive feature distillation for efficient multimodal generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对投机解码在视觉语言模型中应用不足的问题，DREAM-S提出一种针对VLM的投机解码框架，利用神经架构搜索自动识别最佳的草稿-目标模型交互策略和草稿架构，并引入自适应中间特征蒸馏。该方法在保持生成质量的同时显著加速解码，为VLM高效推理提供了新范式。

deep_dive.title: TreeFlash: Parallel AR-Approximation for Faster Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: One-shot block drafters for speculative decoding generate the full draft in a single forward pass, achieving strong throughput by eliminating sequential token generation. However, they predict each draft token conditioned only on the prefix context, with no dependence on previously drafted tokens. This non-autoregressive conditioning causes the drafter's distribution to diverge from the verifier's true autoregressive distribution as draft depth grows. This problem becomes more severe in tree-based drafting, where distinct branches are forced to share the same marginal distribution for subsequent tokens. We propose TreeFlash, which addresses this by incorporating an MLP layer conditioned on the drafter's hidden state and the previous token to approximate an autoregressive distribution. TreeFlash retains the $\mathcal{O}(1)$ decoding time complexity of one-shot drafters by employing a two-stage approximation mechanism. TreeFlash achieves state-of-the-art performance across a variety of tasks and models, improving over marginal tree drafting by $12\%$ higher block efficiency and $9\%$ higher speedup.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: novel speculative decoding method with parallel AR approximation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 新型投机解码方法，并行自回归近似
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 新型投机解码方法，并行自回归近似
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 新型投机解码方法，并行自回归近似
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: TreeFlash introduces a parallel AR-approximation method for speculative decoding, using an MLP layer conditioned on hidden state and previous token to approximate the verifier's distribution, improving draft quality and speed.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有投机解码中的一次性块起草器在树状分支中因非自回归条件导致分布偏差。本文提出TreeFlash，通过在起草器中引入依赖前一token和隐状态的MLP层来近似自回归分布，显著提升草稿质量。在多个LLM基准上，TreeFlash实现了比现有一次性方法更高的加速比，且无需额外训练。

deep_dive.title: Speculative Decoding Across Languages
deep_dive.abstract: Speculative decoding has become a crucial component of large language model (LLM) inference, enabling faster generation by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them in parallel. However, small draft models tend to suffer from disproportionately poor multilingual capabilities. Thus, when generating text in a non-English language, speculative decoding is far less effective.   We compare three strategies to improve speculative decoding efficiency for eleven languages: finetuning the draft model on task-specific data (translation); finetuning the draft model on unlabeled monolingual corpora; and training simple n-gram draft models on the same monolingual corpora. We evaluate efficiency on translation (from English into the target language) and the held-out task of story generation. We find that while task-specific distillation can significantly improve efficiency, distilled models generalize poorly to a new task. Meanwhile, n-gram draft models, despite lower acceptance rates, consistently provide large speed-ups due to much faster draft generation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: studies speculative decoding efficiency for LLMs across languages, directly relevant to LLM inference acceleration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究跨语言场景下大语言模型的投机解码效率，直接相关于LLM推理加速
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究跨语言场景下大语言模型的投机解码效率，直接相关于LLM推理加速
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究跨语言场景下大语言模型的投机解码效率，直接相关于LLM推理加速
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Compares finetuning draft models and n-gram models to improve speculative decoding efficiency for non-English LLM generation, finding finetuning on task-specific data most effective.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对投机解码在多语言LLM推理中效果不佳的问题，比较了三种改进策略：微调草稿模型、单语语料预训练和n-gram模型。实验表明，在翻译任务上使用任务特定数据微调草稿模型效果最好，同时在故事生成任务上也表现出良好的泛化能力。该工作直接推动了LLM推理加速中投机解码技术的实用化。

deep_dive.title: Hybrid Verified Decoding: Learning to Allocate Verification in Speculative Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) generation remains expensive because autoregressive decoding calls the model once for each new token. Speculative decoding reduces this cost by drafting multiple tokens and verifying them with the target model in one step, but its speedup depends on how many drafted tokens are accepted. Parameter-free draft sources can propose long continuations at low cost in structured and agentic workloads, yet a cache match that looks promising at one generation step may have low payoff at the next. We propose Hybrid Verified Decoding, which predicts the accepted length of a cache draft before verification and uses this payoff estimate to choose between cache verification and a model-based drafter. Across three LLMs and sixteen datasets, Hybrid Verified Decoding is especially effective on agentic workflows, where it outperforms EAGLE3 in every setting with a 2.73x average speedup. Our analysis shows how prompt structure creates cache opportunities, how high-payoff cache drafts concentrate in a small part of the draft space, and how payoff-guided selection reduces sequential decoding work, pointing to runtime draft selection as a promising direction for speculative decoding.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: verification allocation strategy in speculative decoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 投机解码中的验证分配策略

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 投机解码中的验证分配策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Hybrid Verified Decoding predicts accepted draft length to choose between cache verification and model-based drafter, accelerating LLM inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 投机解码通过草稿验证加速LLM生成，但如何分配验证资源是关键。本文提出混合验证解码，预先预测草稿被接受的长度，据此在缓存验证和模型起草器之间动态选择，避免低效验证。在多种LLM和工作负载上，该方法显著提升解码加速比，为投机解码提供智能验证分配方案。

deep_dive.title: LocateAnything: Fast and High-Quality Vision-Language Grounding with Parallel Box Decoding
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) commonly formulate visual grounding and detection as a coordinate-token generation problem, serializing each 2D box into multiple 1D tokens that are learned and decoded largely independently. This token-by-token decoding mismatches the coupled structure of box geometry and creates a practical inference bottleneck due to strictly sequential generation. We introduce LocateAnything, a unified generative grounding and detection framework based on Parallel Box Decoding (PBD). By decoding geometric elements such as bounding boxes and points as atomic units in a single step, LocateAnything preserves intra-box geometric coherence and unlocks substantial parallelism. We show that PBD improves both decoding throughput and localization accuracy. We further develop a scalable data engine and curate LocateAnything-Data, a large-scale dataset with more than 138 million training samples, substantially increasing data diversity for high-precision localization. Extensive evaluations show that LocateAnything advances the speed-accuracy frontier, achieving significantly higher decoding throughput while improving high-IoU localization quality across diverse benchmarks. The results highlight the complementary benefits of Parallel Box Decoding and large-scale training data in enabling efficient and precise unified visual grounding and detection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: parallel box decoding for VLM grounding, improves inference throughput
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 并行框解码加速VLM基础模型推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 并行框解码加速VLM基础模型推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 并行框解码加速VLM基础模型推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LocateAnything uses Parallel Box Decoding to decode bounding boxes in one step, boosting VLM inference throughput and preserving geometric coherence.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉语言模型将目标检测转化为序列坐标令牌生成，但串行解码造成几何结构破坏和推理瓶颈。本文提出LocateAnything框架，采用并行框解码（PBD）技术，将边界框和点作为原子单元单步解码，保持几何一致性并实现高度并行化。实验表明，PBD显著提高解码吞吐量，同时保持检测精度，为VLM实时应用提供高效基础。

deep_dive.title: CIVIC: End-to-End Sequence Compactness for Efficient Vision-Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) face severe memory and latency bottlenecks due to high-resolution visual tokens. While current token reduction methods theoretically save FLOPs, post-hoc pruning introduces structural overhead, failing to yield proportional wall-clock acceleration. However, enforcing a contiguous compact pathway risks geometric disorientation and loss of fine-grained localization. To overcome these barriers, this paper introduces CIVIC, a path-consistent compact visual inference framework. By maintaining compact sequence representations seamlessly across the vision encoder, projection layer, LLM prefill, and KV-cache, CIVIC avoids non-contiguous memory access and localized unmerging overheads. Evaluated on the Qwen3-VL architecture, CIVIC successfully translates sequence reductions into genuine physical hardware efficiency, shrinking KV-cache memory to approximately one-third of the baseline and reducing end-to-end inference latency. Enabled by text-aligned KL distillation and an adaptive spatial retention floor, CIVIC achieves these efficiency milestones without degrading accuracy across rigorous multimodal reasoning and visual grounding benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: end-to-end sequence compactness for efficient VLM inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 端到端序列紧凑性加速VLM推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 端到端序列紧凑性加速VLM推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 端到端序列紧凑性加速VLM推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CIVIC maintains compact visual token sequences across VLM encoder, projector, prefill and KV-cache, achieving wall-clock speedup.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉语言模型因高分辨率视觉令牌面临严重内存和延迟瓶颈。现有令牌减少方法后置剪枝带来结构开销，无法实现墙钟加速。本文提出CIVIC框架，在视觉编码器、投影层、LLM预填充和KV缓存之间保持连贯紧凑序列，避免非连续内存访问和局部合并。在Qwen3-VL上评估，显著降低推理延迟和内存使用，并为VLM高效推理提供新范式。

quick_skim.title: PARCEL: Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding
quick_skim.abstract: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) map visual inputs into dense token sequences, imposing a quadratic computational bottleneck for inference. Elastic visual-token compression addresses this by training a single model that can run at multiple visual-token budgets. However, existing approaches struggle under aggressive compression. Spatial-only compression, as in nested pooling, behaves as an imperfect low-pass filter and induces spectral aliasing that obscures fine-grained detail. Query-only compression, as in nested query resampling, replaces explicit grid-aligned tokens with non-local summaries and substantially degrades spatial grounding. To resolve this representational conflict, we introduce PARCEL (Pool-Anchored Resampling with Conditioned Elastic Queries for Efficient Vision-Language Understanding), a visual tokenization architecture that dynamically partitions the labor of feature extraction. PARCEL establishes spatial pool tokens as low-frequency layout anchors and conditions elastic query tokens on these anchors through Pool-Conditioned Query Resampling. This encourages query tokens to focus on complementary visual features rather than redundant spatial mapping. Extensive evaluations across 27 benchmarks show that PARCEL improves the performance-efficiency Pareto frontier, consistently outperforming existing matryoshka baselines across visual-token budgets while preserving the "train once, deploy anywhere" paradigm.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Elastic visual-token compression for efficient VLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 弹性视觉令牌压缩用于高效VLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 弹性视觉令牌压缩用于高效VLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 弹性视觉令牌压缩用于高效VLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PARCEL introduces a pool-anchored resampling method with conditioned elastic queries that resolves representational conflict between spatial and query compression, enabling aggressive visual token reduction while preserving spatial grounding, thus accelerating VLM inference without quality loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大视觉语言模型推理时视觉令牌序列过长导致计算瓶颈的问题，本文提出PARCEL方法。该方法通过锚定池重采样与条件弹性查询，解决了空间压缩与查询压缩的表示冲突。实验表明，在激进压缩下仍能保持空间定位能力，显著加速推理且不损失性能。该方法为高效VLM推理提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: ETC: Extreme Token Compression via Task-aware Visual Information Distillation in VLMs
quick_skim.abstract: In Vision-Language Models (VLMs), high-resolution images produce a large number of visual tokens, resulting in high computational costs and KV-cache overhead during inference. To address this problem, we propose an Extreme Token Compression (ETC) framework that minimizes task loss when reducing the number of input tokens based on the principle of variational information distillation. Specifically, from an information-theoretic perspective, we show that minimizing task loss requires the compact representation to preserve the instruction-aware sufficient statistic of the task-relevant visual information for prediction. In practice, ETC leverages text-to-image cross-attention to weight the original visual features to approximate the latent instruction-aware predictive statistic. Moreover, ETC introduces a variational information distillation, enabling the compact representation to preserve the essential information to recover this predictive statistic. Experiments on LLaVA-1.5-7B and Qwen3-VL-2B show that ETC remains effective even under single-token compression, substantially reducing KV-cache overhead while retaining strong task performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: vision language model inference acceleration via extreme token compression and KV-cache reduction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过极端令牌压缩和KV缓存减少加速视觉语言模型推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过极端令牌压缩和KV缓存减少加速视觉语言模型推理

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ETC framework uses variational information distillation to compress visual tokens, reducing inference cost and KV-cache overhead while preserving task-relevant information for VLMs.

quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) commonly formulate visual grounding and detection as a coordinate-token generation problem, serializing each 2D box into multiple 1D tokens that are learned and decoded largely independently. This token-by-token decoding mismatches the coupled structure of box geometry and creates a practical inference bottleneck due to strictly sequential generation. We introduce LocateAnything, a unified generative grounding and detection framework based on Parallel Box Decoding (PBD). By decoding geometric elements such as bounding boxes and points as atomic units in a single step, LocateAnything preserves intra-box geometric coherence and unlocks substantial parallelism. quick_skim.title: VisionPulse: Dynamic Visual Sparsity for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning
quick_skim.abstract: With the rapid advancement of large multimodal models (LMMs), inference-time overhead has become a key bottleneck for real-world deployment. Existing methods typically prune visual tokens at prefill, assuming the required visual evidence remains static during reasoning. However, we empirically show that visual evidence is strongly step-dependent: only a sparse subset of visual tokens is critical at each decoding step, and the critical set evolves across reasoning. Furthermore, we identify a coupled bottleneck where redundant visual context can steer the model toward query-irrelevant regions, lengthening the reasoning trace. Guided by these insights, we propose VisionPulse, a step-wise visual token pruning framework during reasoning. VisionPulse computes a lightweight visual attention mass to estimate the step-wise retention budget by exploiting its strong positive correlation with LMMs' effective visual token usage and retain only the most critical tokens under this budget. By enforcing visual sparsity during reasoning, VisionPulse filters redundant visual context while preserving relevant visual evidence, shortening reasoning traces naturally. Extensive experiments show that VisionPulse only retains 5% of visual tokens per step with reasoning traces shortened by 11.2%, while keeping accuracy almost unchanged.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: dynamic visual sparsity for efficient multimodal reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 动态视觉稀疏性实现高效多模态推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 动态视觉稀疏性实现高效多模态推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 动态视觉稀疏性实现高效多模态推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VisionPulse proposes step-wise visual token pruning during multimodal reasoning, dynamically adapting the critical visual set per decoding step to reduce overhead and avoid distracting the model.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大型多模态模型推理开销成为部署瓶颈。VisionPulse发现视觉证据是步骤依赖的，每个解码步骤只需稀疏的视觉令牌且关键集动态变化，冗余视觉上下文会误导推理。为此提出VisionPulse，一种推理时逐步视觉令牌剪枝框架，在每个解码步骤动态保留关键视觉令牌。实验表明，该方法有效降低计算开销并提升推理准确性，为多模态模型高效推理提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: EvoCut: Multi-Layer Evolution-Aware Visual Token Compression for Efficient Large Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) achieve strong performance on image and video understanding tasks, but their inference efficiency is constrained by the large number of visual tokens produced by vision encoders. Most existing visual token compression methods estimate token importance from attention scores or representation properties at specific layers, overlooking how visual tokens evolve across the vision encoder. Such layer-specific criteria may provide incomplete importance estimates and limit performance preservation after compression. To address this issue, we analyze layer-wise visual token evolution directions and observe that tokens form multiple group evolution directions across vision-encoder layers. Our analysis further shows that informative tokens tend to exhibit persistent deviations from common group evolution directions. Based on this observation, we propose EvoCut, a training-free and attention-free visual token compression method that estimates token importance from multi-layer evolution deviation. Experimental results show that EvoCut can retain only 11.1\% of the visual tokens on LLaVA-1.5-7B while preserving 94.4\% of the average performance, demonstrating its effectiveness in balancing efficiency and accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: visual token compression for efficient LVLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 视觉token压缩提升LVLM推理效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 视觉token压缩提升LVLM推理效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 视觉token压缩提升LVLM推理效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: EvoCut introduces multi-layer evolution-aware visual token compression, analyzing token evolution across encoder layers to selectively prune tokens, accelerating LVLM inference with minimal performance loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大型视觉语言模型(LVLM)推理效率受限于视觉编码器产生的大量token。现有压缩方法仅关注单层重要性，忽视token跨层演化。本文提出EvoCut，通过分析token在编码器各层的演化方向，识别并保留关键token，实现高效压缩。实验表明，EvoCut在保持下游任务性能的同时显著加速推理。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大型视觉语言模型(LVLM)推理效率受限于视觉编码器产生的大量token。现有压缩方法仅关注单层重要性，忽视token跨层演化。本文提出EvoCut，通过分析token在编码器各层的演化方向，识别并保留关键token，实现高效压缩。实验表明，EvoCut在保持下游任务性能的同时显著加速推理。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Surrogate Gradients: Fully Differentiable Token Pruning for Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Visual token pruning reduces the computational cost of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by removing redundant visual tokens. Existing methods typically rely on Gumbel-Softmax to approximate discrete selection during training. However, the optimization is driven by surrogate gradients rather than the true selection process, leading to unreliable learning of token importance. In this paper, we propose DiffPrune, which reformulates pruning as continuous control of token information instead of discrete selection learning. Specifically, we introduce an Information Throttler that modulates each token using variance-preserving noise conditioned on importance scores, where higher scores induce less information suppression during training. This design directly operates on token representations, naturally providing a fully differentiable optimization path for learning token importance. At inference, tokens are removed via hard thresholding on the learned scores. Across ten VLM benchmarks, DiffPrune retains 96.5% of full-model accuracy while accelerating LLM prefill by 2.85x, with only 0.69 ms of inference overhead.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: fully differentiable token pruning for VLM, avoiding surrogate gradients, improving training and inference efficiency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 完全可微的视觉令牌剪枝，避免代理梯度，提升训练和推理效率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 完全可微的视觉令牌剪枝，避免代理梯度，提升训练和推理效率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 完全可微的视觉令牌剪枝，避免代理梯度，提升训练和推理效率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DiffPrune reformulates token pruning as continuous information control, using variance-preserving noise to modulate tokens, removing reliance on Gumbel-Softmax surrogate gradients for VLM efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有视觉令牌剪枝依赖Gumbel-Softmax近似离散选择，代理梯度导致重要性学习不可靠。本文提出DiffPrune，将剪枝重新定义为连续信息控制：通过基于重要性分数的保方差噪声调制令牌信息，训练时高重要性令牌信息抑制少。此法避免了代理梯度，剪枝更稳定，在保持VLM性能的同时有效降低计算成本，为VLM高效推理提供新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有视觉令牌剪枝依赖Gumbel-Softmax近似离散选择，代理梯度导致重要性学习不可靠。本文提出DiffPrune，将剪枝重新定义为连续信息控制：通过基于重要性分数的保方差噪声调制令牌信息，训练时高重要性令牌信息抑制少。此法避免了代理梯度，剪枝更稳定，在保持VLM性能的同时有效降低计算成本，为VLM高效推理提供新范式。

quick_skim.title: Improving Visual Token Reduction via Rectifying Distortions for Efficient Multimodal LLM Inference
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advancements in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in vision-language tasks, yet the quadratic computational complexity arising from the vast number of visual tokens incurs significant memory and latency bottlenecks. While visual token reduction (VTR) strategies have been explored to mitigate this burden, existing methods overlook the positional and attentional consistency between the full and reduced sequences, resulting in a distorted representation. To this end, we propose RESTORE, a novel VTR framework that rectifies the positional and attentional distortions while maintaining efficiency. Specifically, we present a simple yet effective calibration method that restores lost visual attention by augmenting attention weights based on relative distances. We also introduce a distinctive anchor selection for token merging to mitigate information loss during feature averaging. Experimental results on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that our method consistently improves the accuracy of various reduction methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance while maintaining computational efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Visual token reduction with distortion rectification for efficient MLLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过纠正失真的视觉令牌减少实现高效多模态大语言模型推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过纠正失真的视觉令牌减少实现高效多模态大语言模型推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过纠正失真的视觉令牌减少实现高效多模态大语言模型推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RESTORE framework rectifies positional and attentional distortions during visual token reduction, restoring lost visual information and maintaining consistency, enabling efficient MLLM inference with minimal accuracy loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多模态大语言模型中视觉令牌减少导致表示失真进而影响性能的问题，本文提出RESTORE框架。该框架通过校准位置与注意力的一致性来恢复丢失的视觉信息。实验表明，RESTORE在显著减少令牌数的同时，有效维持了模型性能，为高效MLLM推理提供了一种有效的折中方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多模态大语言模型中视觉令牌减少导致表示失真进而影响性能的问题，本文提出RESTORE框架。该框架通过校准位置与注意力的一致性来恢复丢失的视觉信息。实验表明，RESTORE在显著减少令牌数的同时，有效维持了模型性能，为高效MLLM推理提供了一种有效的折中方案。

quick_skim.title: Cost-Aware Diffusion Draft Trees for Speculative Decoding
quick_skim.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates inference by having a lightweight drafter propose tokens verified in parallel by the target language model. Block diffusion drafters such as DFlash generate an entire draft block in one pass, yielding per-position marginals; DDTree uses these to build a candidate tree that maximizes expected acceptance length under a fixed node budget. We observe, however, that acceptance length is non-decreasing in budget: it always favors larger trees regardless of verification cost, offering no principled basis for budget selection. We introduce \textbf{CaDDTree} (Cost-aware Diffusion Draft Tree), a method that directly optimizes token throughput (expected tokens generated per unit time) by jointly selecting the tree structure and node budget. We model draft and verification latencies explicitly, show that the throughput objective decomposes into a per-round one-dimensional search over the budget, and prove that under a convex verification cost the throughput function is \emph{unimodal}, enabling an efficient greedy stopping rule. CaDDTree requires no offline budget search, adapting the budget each round from the current per-position distributions and verification cost. Experiments on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B across eight benchmarks spanning reasoning, coding, and instruction-following tasks show that \caDDTree{} matches or surpasses DDTree with oracle budget selection on nearly all tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cost-aware diffusion draft trees for speculative decoding in LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LLM投机解码的成本感知扩散草稿树
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向LLM投机解码的成本感知扩散草稿树
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向LLM投机解码的成本感知扩散草稿树
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CaDDTree optimizes token throughput for speculative decoding by jointly selecting tree structure and node budget, addressing the issue that acceptance length is non-decreasing in budget.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有投机解码草稿树方法忽略验证代价，导致预算选择缺乏依据。CaDDTree提出成本感知扩散草稿树，通过联合优化树结构和节点预算，直接最大化令牌吞吐量。该方法在保持精度的同时提升了解码效率，为LLM投机解码提供了成本感知的优化视角。

quick_skim.title: PrunePath: Towards Highly Structured Sparse Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Feed-forward networks (FFNs) dominate the parameter count and computation of modern language models, yet existing pruning methods often struggle to convert sparsity into hardware-friendly inference efficiency gains. We introduce \textbf{PrunePath}, a budget-adaptive structured sparsification framework for FFN layers. Built on MoEfication, PrunePath replaces independent expert-wise thresholding with a softmax-normalized routing distribution and activates important experts under a cumulative-mass threshold. This formulation imposes a token-level probability budget, enabling adaptive expert counts and a direct inference-time sparsity knob from a single checkpoint. Across NLU, NLG, and instruction-tuning evaluations, PrunePath achieves a favorable sparsity--performance trade-off compared with existing static pruning and MoEfication-based methods. We further implement Triton kernels for KV-cache decoding to translate the resulting structured sparsity into practical memory savings and measurable decoding-speed improvements. These results demonstrate the superior performance of PrunePath for building highly sparse, deployment-friendly large language models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structured pruning for efficient LLM inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 结构化剪枝用于高效LLM推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 结构化剪枝用于高效LLM推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 结构化剪枝用于高效LLM推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PrunePath introduces a budget-adaptive structured sparsification for FFN layers in LLMs, enabling direct inference-time sparsity control via a single checkpoint and achieving favorable sparsity-performance trade-offs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对前馈网络参数和计算占比大的问题，PrunePath提出了一种基于MoEfication的预算自适应结构化稀疏框架，通过软最大化归一化路由分布和累积阈值激活专家，实现自适应专家数量和推理时稀疏度控制。实验表明，该方法在自然语言理解、生成和指令微调上取得了良好的稀疏-性能平衡，有助于提升LLM推理效率。

quick_skim.title: DeepLatent: Think with Images via Parallel Latent Visual Reasoning
quick_skim.abstract: The emerging paradigm of "thinking with images" embeds visual states into intermediate reasoning steps, defining a new frontier for Vision-Language Models. Existing approaches diverge along two lines. Tool-assisted methods apply explicit visual operations but suffer from high latency and restricted manipulation types. Latent reasoning methods autoregressively produce implicit visual states, but underperform tool-assisted methods, and their latent tokens fail to capture effective visual information. In this work, we propose DeepLatent, a parallel framework for latent visual reasoning. First, we introduce LatentFormer. It uses learnable 2D tokens to generate context-conditioned latent states in parallel, anchoring every visual update directly in the original image features. Second, we design a continuous-space reinforcement learning algorithm. It optimizes latent modulation parameters directly in the embedding space, significantly improving latent representation quality. The framework is trained via knowledge distillation followed by this continuous-space RL algorithm. Furthermore, we contribute DeepLatent-180K, a large-scale dataset tailored for latent visual reasoning. Extensive evaluations across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that DeepLatent achieves state-of-the-art performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes parallel latent visual reasoning to reduce latency in VLM reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出并行潜在视觉推理以减少VLM推理延迟
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出并行潜在视觉推理以减少VLM推理延迟
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出并行潜在视觉推理以减少VLM推理延迟
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces DeepLatent, a parallel framework for latent visual reasoning that generates context-conditioned latent states in parallel using learnable 2D tokens, reducing latency compared to autoregressive methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作针对现有VLM视觉推理方法延迟高的问题，提出DeepLatent并行潜在视觉推理框架。通过引入LatentFormer以可学习2D令牌并行生成上下文条件潜在状态，避免了自回归步骤。实验表明该方法在保持推理质量的同时显著降低了延迟，为VLM推理加速提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该工作针对现有VLM视觉推理方法延迟高的问题，提出DeepLatent并行潜在视觉推理框架。通过引入LatentFormer以可学习2D令牌并行生成上下文条件潜在状态，避免了自回归步骤。实验表明该方法在保持推理质量的同时显著降低了延迟，为VLM推理加速提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Mitigating Hallucinations in Large Language Models Via Decoder Layer Skipping
quick_skim.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved strong performance across diverse natural language tasks, yet their outputs often suffer from hallucinations -- content that is misaligned with factual information. In this work, we conduct a comprehensive layer-wise analysis of the decoding process and reveal that hallucinations tend to originate from deeper decoder layers. To address this issue, we introduce \textbf{DeLask} (\textbf{De}coder \textbf{La}yer \textbf{Sk}ipping), a novel decoding framework that dynamically skips layers prone to producing hallucinations. DeLask leverages the theoretical insight that the forward computation of an $L$-layer Transformer is conditionally equivalent to $L$ steps of gradient descent. We define a \emph{driftance value} by computing the cosine similarity between gradients derived from consecutive decoder steps, identifying problematic layers when the descent direction reverses. Rather than discarding such layers entirely, DeLask partially aggregates their hidden states with preceding layers, thereby preserving consistency while suppressing erroneous signals. Extensive experiments across diverse LLMs and benchmarks demonstrate that DeLask consistently mitigates hallucinations and enhances overall reliability, providing a lightweight and generalizable decoding framework for improving the robustness of large-scale language models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Decoder layer skipping for efficient LLM inference and hallucination mitigation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 解码器层跳过实现高效LLM推理与幻觉缓解
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 解码器层跳过实现高效LLM推理与幻觉缓解
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 解码器层跳过实现高效LLM推理与幻觉缓解
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DeLask dynamically skips hallucination-prone deeper decoder layers during LLM decoding, leveraging gradient descent equivalence, reducing computational cost while maintaining output quality and reducing hallucinations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型深层解码层易产生幻觉且计算成本高的问题，本文提出DeLask框架。它基于Transformer前向计算与梯度下降的等价性，动态跳过可能产生幻觉的层。实验结果表明，DeLask在减少推理计算量的同时，有效降低了幻觉率，并保持了文本质量。该方法为LLM高效推理与可控生成提供了新途径。
